description: Execute a plan until it is complete

steps.label: Planner Select And Store Next Plan

content: Use fresh disk reads and current git state, not conversational memory. Re-read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`, and determine the active `plan_path`, then reopen that exact relative `plan_path` from disk even if you have read it before, using explicit shell reads such as `sed`, `cat`, or `rg`. Recheck current repository branch state directly from git, for example with `git branch --show-current`, and check the most recent commit directly from git, for example with `git log --oneline -1`. You MUST double-check whether another agent has edited the plan or changed its scope since you last looked at it. You MUST read the whole plan again and CANNOT assume you understand it from before. Once you have done this, give me a summary of what the plan entails.

Do you have any new requirement/scope based questions about the plan that need clarification before the plan is tasked up or worked on? Even if you have previously provided questions, maybe you have thought of some more after re-reading the plan and asking yourself `Am I sure about the full plan scope and requirements, and is it all documented within the plan`? If a '## Questions' section doesn't already exist within the plan, then create one at the end of the file, even if you do not have any questions to add. If you have questions not already in the section, then add them to the '## Questions' section as a numbered list. If the section already contains questions, then please renumber them so that the full list is ordered numerically and starts from 1. If you do not have any new questions you MUST write the exact line '- No Further Questions' immediately below the '## Questions' title, and above any existing questions within the section.

steps.label: Check for No Further Questions
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk before answering. Did you add additional/new questions to the plan file during your latest edit (adding 'No Further Questions' does NOT count as adding questions)? You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Check for No Further Questions
steps.question: Run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/questions_section_status.py"`. Answer yes only if the JSON output reports `has_no_further_questions_line: true`. Answer no otherwise. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

content: Please read through each question listed within the '## Questions' section of the plan as it should have been edited by another agent. For each question, I want you to perform thorough research for each question separately in order. While researching an answer, I want you to follow these golden rules: 1. Use the KISS principle, only planning what is needed for THIS story and avoiding extra work and over engineering. Follow established patterns of what has been done before. 3. Ensure we are not going outside the scope of the original plan. 4. Ensure everything we are planning to do has a purpose - To answer each question you MUST follow these steps: 1. Use the code_info tool to find out how we have approached similar issues within this repository before.  2. Use the code_info tool to find out how these kinds of issues have been approached within ALL ingested repositories. 3. Use the deepwiki and context7 tools along with web searches to confirm any questions related to external libraries that are not accessible from the code_info tool.  4. Search the web to see if other people have asked the same question, and use the best and proven solutions that match with our KISS principles.  - Once you have an answer to a question, add it to a '## Decisions' section immediately after the '## Questions' section (you will need to create it if it doesn't already exist). Each decision should be added as an item within a numbered list under the '## Decisions' section. The decision should indicate: - the question being addressed, WHY the question matters, WHAT the answer is, WHERE the answer came from and WHY it is the best answer to the question. In addition to adding the answer to the '## Decisions' section, you MUST update all other sections of the plan where appropriate based on the information from the answer, including description, acceptance, out of scope, etc... You MUST ALSO remove answered questions from the '## Questions' section, but never actually remove the '## Questions' section heading (in case more questions are added later). If it is impossible for you to answer a question after following the above steps, just leave it within the '## Questions' section. Never task up and commit after making any changes.

steps.label: Refresh current plan handoff after planning

content: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`, and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Then commit all local changes, and if possible, git push the story branch in the current repository and in every repository listed in `additional_repositories`.

steps.label: Refresh current plan handoff after improve plan

content: Use fresh disk reads and current git state, not conversational memory. Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`, and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Reopen the exact relative `plan_path` from disk now, using explicit shell reads such as `sed`, `cat`, or `rg`, and read the whole selected plan again before judging the tasking output, because another agent has just edited it. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. The plan has now been tasked up. Confirm the tasking output against the freshly re-read plan on disk, including any new tasks, subtasks, testing sections, manual-testing guidance, documentation locations, and the simple story that were just added or updated. Please check that the tasks created fully cover that selected plan and that nothing has been missed. Ensure the tasking followed the current task-up rules, including portable non-absolute paths outside concrete local `Additional Repositories` entries, test-only auth or login enablement, final lint then prettier subtasks, final lint then prettier testing steps, manual-testing preference for the unmodified human Docker stack when feasible, ignored artifact paths for automated screenshots, consistent `codeInfoTmp/` and `codeInfoStatus/` casing, and a `.gitignore` entry for `codeInfoTmp/` whenever manual testing for the story writes task-level proof artifacts into that scratch path. If you feel something is missing, please update the plan, tasks, and simple story accordingly, and if you make changes in this audit step then create a follow-up commit for those edits before finishing. Remember to ensure that each task has clear implementation instructions and testing steps so a developer can fully understand what to do. Please confirm once you are happy that the plan has been fully tasked up and nothing is missing. Do not ask me any questions, instead just update the plan and tasks following the KISS principle until you are happy everything is fully covered.

steps.label: Refresh current plan handoff after tasking

content: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`, and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Reopen the exact relative `plan_path` from disk, for example with `sed`, `cat`, or `rg`, before answering so you are not relying on memory. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. Then check the last commit in the current repository and in every additional repository AND re-read that same plan, even if you have read it before. You MUST double-check whether tasks have been added to it by another agent or whether anything has changed. You must ensure you fully understand the plan and its remaining tasks before proceeding. You MUST read the plan and CANNOT assume you understand it from before. You MUST read the whole plan again even if you have read it before. Once you have done this, give me a summary of the remaining work.

steps.label: Task Execution And Blocker Repair Loop
Exit if no valid implementation task handoff is available
steps.question: Answer from the selector result you just produced in the immediately preceding step. Answer yes only if that step did NOT leave one valid current task handoff for implementation, for example because selector stdout and the reread current-task.json disagreed, the current task handoff must be regenerated, plan repair is needed, or the story is already complete. Answer no only if the selector stdout result and the persisted current-task.json agreed on one resolved current task for this loop pass. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Proceed to automated proof if implementation is complete
steps.question: Use the same bound task from the immediately preceding implementation audit step. If that step did not clearly resolve one bound task, answer no. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <that-same-bound-task-number>`. Answer yes only when `selected_task` exists, `selected_task.has_unchecked_subtasks` is false, and `selected_task.live_blockers` is empty. Answer no otherwise. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Implementation blocker remains
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json`, and determine the bound task from what it contains rather than depending on an exact JSON shape. If it does not clearly resolve a task, answer yes. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <bound-task-number>`. Answer yes iff `selected_task` is missing OR `selected_task.live_blockers` is non-empty. Answer no otherwise. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Exit current task loop if implementation left a blocker
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json`, and determine the bound task from what it contains rather than depending on an exact JSON shape. If it does not clearly resolve a task, answer yes. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <bound-task-number>`. Answer yes iff `selected_task` is missing OR `selected_task.live_blockers` is non-empty. Answer no otherwise. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Re-embed plan-scope repositories before automated proof
Run automated proof and fix issues
Proceed to manual testing eligibility if automated proof is complete
steps.question: Use the same bound task from the immediately preceding automated-proof audit step. If that step did not clearly resolve one bound task, answer no. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <that-same-bound-task-number>`. Answer yes only when `selected_task` exists, `selected_task.has_unchecked_subtasks` is false, `selected_task.has_unchecked_testing` is false, and `selected_task.live_blockers` is empty. Answer no otherwise. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Return to implementation from proof loop
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json`, and determine the bound task from what it contains rather than depending on an exact JSON shape. If it does not clearly resolve a task, answer no. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <bound-task-number>`. Answer yes iff `selected_task` exists and `selected_task.has_unchecked_subtasks` is true. Answer no otherwise. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Automated-proof blocker remains
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json`, and determine the bound task from what it contains rather than depending on an exact JSON shape. If it does not clearly resolve a task, answer yes. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <bound-task-number>`. Answer yes iff `selected_task` is missing OR `selected_task.live_blockers` is non-empty. Answer no otherwise. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Exit current task loop if task is blocked after proof
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json`, and determine the bound task from what it contains rather than depending on an exact JSON shape. If it does not clearly resolve a task, answer yes. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <bound-task-number>`. Answer yes iff `selected_task` is missing OR `selected_task.live_blockers` is non-empty. Answer no otherwise. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Return to the top of current task rework if proof added unchecked subtasks
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json`, and determine the bound task from what it contains rather than depending on an exact JSON shape. If it does not clearly resolve a task, answer no. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <bound-task-number>`. Answer yes iff `selected_task` exists, `selected_task.has_unchecked_subtasks` is true, and `selected_task.live_blockers` is empty. Answer no otherwise. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Return to the top of current task rework if automated proof still has unchecked testing
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json`, and determine the bound task from what it contains rather than depending on an exact JSON shape. If it does not clearly resolve a task, answer no. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <bound-task-number>`. Answer yes iff `selected_task` exists, `selected_task.has_unchecked_subtasks` is false, `selected_task.has_unchecked_testing` is true, and `selected_task.live_blockers` is empty. Answer no otherwise. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Re-embed plan-scope repositories before manual-testing eligibility review
Reconfirm repository-defined manual testing skip policy

content: Before manual-testing work continues, re-check `AGENTS.md` and `codeinfo_markdown/repository_information.md` for any repository-defined conditions that allow manual proof to be narrowed or skipped. Follow those repository-owned rules if they are being met during proof. Do not invent your own skip reasons, and do not reopen the task or add implementation work solely because a repository-defined skip condition was hit.

steps.label: Check for blocker after manual testing
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-task.json`, and determine the bound task from what it contains rather than depending on an exact JSON shape. If it does not clearly resolve a task, answer no. Otherwise run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_status.py" --task-number <bound-task-number>`. Answer from that JSON output only. Answer yes iff `selected_task` exists and `selected_task.live_blockers` is non-empty. The parser already ignores inline `**BLOCKER**` references, `**BLOCKING ANSWER**`, and `**RESOLVED ISSUE**`. Reply only as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Exit task loop if story is complete
steps.question: Run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/story_workflow_status.py"`. Answer yes only if the JSON output reports `repair_needed: false`, `scope_valid: true`, and `story_complete: true`. Answer no otherwise. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Early exit check for completion
steps.question: Run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/story_workflow_status.py"`. Answer yes only if the JSON output reports `repair_needed: false`, `scope_valid: true`, and `story_complete: true`. Answer no otherwise. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Re-embed plan-scope repositories before active-task normalization
Refresh current plan handoff after normalization
Research blocker solution and prove it
Refresh current plan handoff after planner edits

content: Run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/story_workflow_status.py" --include-tasks` and use that JSON output as the source of truth. Before answering completion, list each task's Task Status from `tasks`, explicitly state whether the final task is `__done__` or not using `final_task_status`, cite `plan_path`, and state whether `scope_valid`, `repair_needed`, and `repair_action` are true, false, or null.

steps.label: Check for completion
steps.question: Run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/story_workflow_status.py"`. Answer yes only if the JSON output reports `repair_needed: false`, `scope_valid: true`, and `story_complete: true`. Answer no otherwise. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Re-embed plan-scope repositories before review disposition
Re-embed plan-scope repositories before minor review fixes
Exit Minor-Fix Path Unless Minor Findings Remain
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json`, and answer only from that file's current contents. Answer yes if `needs_minor_fix_path` is not true. Answer no only when `needs_minor_fix_path` is true, meaning unresolved minor-batchable findings still remain and should continue to be attempted inline before task-up. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Exit Minor-Fix Path After Sync Unless Another Minor Finding Remains
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk after the sync step, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json`, and read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/minor-review-fix-result.json` from disk too, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/minor-review-fix-result.json`. Answer only from those freshly re-read files. Answer yes iff `needs_minor_fix_path` is not true, meaning no unresolved minor-batchable findings remain or the remaining minor findings were bulk-escalated out of the minor bucket. Answer no only if `needs_minor_fix_path` is true, meaning another unresolved minor-batchable finding still needs an inline attempt in this same pass. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Re-embed plan-scope repositories before review task-up
Exit Task-Up Path Unless Unresolved Findings Need Tasks
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json`, and answer only from that file's current contents. Answer yes iff `needs_task_up_path` is not true. Treat `resolved_minor_findings` as non-actionable for task-up. Answer no only when `needs_task_up_path` is true, meaning unresolved task-required findings or incomplete-review blockers must be encoded into the plan. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Ensure Unresolved Review Findings Became Tasks
Refresh Current Plan Handoff After Review Task-Up
Ensure Review-Created Testing Steps Are Correct
Create Or Update Simple Story After Review Task-Up
Exit Task-Up Path After Encoding Review Findings
steps.question: Answer yes after the task-up path has either encoded unresolved review findings into the plan or recorded why that path cannot continue. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"}.

steps.label: Exit Review Loop After Serious Review Work Was Tasked Up
steps.question: Run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/story_workflow_status.py"`. Answer yes only if the JSON output reports `repair_needed: false`, `review_state_repair_needed: false`, and `should_exit_review_loop_to_main_loop: true`. Answer no otherwise. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Exit Review Loop When No Further Minor Rerun Is Needed
steps.question: Run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/story_workflow_status.py"`. Answer yes only if the JSON output reports `repair_needed: false`, `review_state_repair_needed: false`, and `should_finish_review_loop_cleanly: true`. Answer no otherwise. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Generate Or Update Final Revalidation Task For Minor Fixes
Checkpoint Push Before Story Completion Check
Check for completion
steps.question: Run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/story_workflow_status.py"`. Answer yes only if the JSON output reports `scope_valid: true` and `story_complete: true`. Answer no otherwise. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Re-embed plan-scope repositories before promoting story manual proof
